# Signing In with Google or Apple

Source: https://heliumedu.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/159000426995

In addition to signing in with an email and password, Helium supports signing in with your Google or Apple account. This lets you log in without managing a separate password.

## Available Providers

  * **Google** — available on iOS, Android, and web
  * **Apple** — available on iOS and web

## How to Sign In

  1. On the [login screen](https://app.heliumedu.com/login), tap **Sign in with Google** or **Sign in with Apple**
  2. Complete the sign-in prompt from Google or Apple
  3. If this is your first time signing in, Helium will create an account for you and link it to this third-party provider

## Linking an Existing Account

If you already have a Helium account with an email and password, you can link it to your Google or Apple account (as long as the email address matches). Follow the same steps above for "How to Sign In", and when the process completes, Helium will detect your existing account by the matching email address, and merge them.

Once linked, you can sign in using either your email and password _or_ Google/Apple—whichever is more convenient.

## Account Management with OAuth

If your account was created entirely through Google or Apple sign-in (no password was set), the **Change Password** option will not appear in Settings, and **Forgot Password** doesn't do anything. Password management for those accounts is handled through the respective provider (Google/Apple).

If your account has a linked OAuth provider, the **Change Email** option will not appear in Settings.

If your account is linked to both a password and an OAuth provider, the Change Password option remains available.

## Related Articles

  * [Resetting Your Password](https://heliumedu.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/159000418671)

---

## Helium Classic

Helium Classic supports email and password sign-in only. Google and Apple sign-in are not available in Helium Classic.

__Helium Classic will remain available until July 31, 2026.__
